questions.text: Dark clouds gather and the picnic plan changes at the last minute. Everyone goes indoors. What helps the day still feel meaningful?

options.text: Adjust plans and create a new kind of fun
Sit and wait for the rain to stop
Keep thinking about what was missed

questions.text: Heavy rain slows the journey, and the bus barely moves. What choice supports calm thinking during the delay?

options.text: Keep checking the clock again and again
Build frustration by thinking of delays
Notice surroundings and pass time mindfully

questions.text: Rain pours during lunch break, and a student realizes their food is missing. What response helps the body and mood?

options.text: Spend the break worrying about hunger
Look for a safe and helpful solution nearby
Stay silent even if help is possible

questions.text: Thunder cuts the power while a family is watching a movie. The room turns quiet. What keeps the moment comfortable?

options.text: Turn the pause into shared conversation

questions.text: After rain, the usual ride is missed and plans shift. What mindset helps the start of the day feel steady?

options.text: Accept the change and move at a calm pace
